attributes.description: Alerts when the number of files at L0 in the Pebble LSM tree exceeds 1000 on a store (`storage_l0_num_files > 1000`). This is a canonical sign that compactions are falling behind and read amplification is about to degrade.
attributes.name: [CockroachDB OTel] L0 file count high

investigation_guide.blob:
## CockroachDB L0 file count high

### What fired
`storage_l0_num_files` on one or more stores exceeded 1000 over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
L0 is the top of the Pebble LSM tree. Files land here from in-memory flushes and are compacted down into L1+. When L0 grows past ~1000 files, read amplification rises sharply and compaction debt becomes self-reinforcing. Left unchecked, the store will begin stalling writes.

### Triage
1. Open the Storage dashboard filtered by `attributes.node_id` / `attributes.store`.
2. Correlate with `storage_l0_sublevels` (sub-levels growing?), `rocksdb_read_amplification` (rising?), `rocksdb_compactions` rate (stalling or thrashing?).
3. Check admission control (`admission_io_overload`) and disk queue (`sys_host_disk_iopsinprogress`).
4. Inspect CPU — compactions are CPU-heavy and will be squeezed out of a hot node.

### Remediation
- Reduce write throughput to the store until compactions catch up.
- Scale out to redistribute writes across more stores.
- Investigate disk speed; faster disks finish compactions faster.

### Tuning
- Default threshold (`> 1000`, 15-minute window) is conservative. For latency-sensitive clusters, tighten to `> 500`.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-cockroachdb.otel-default*
| WHERE service.name == "cockroachdb"
  AND storage_l0_num_files IS NOT NULL
  AND storage_l0_num_files > 1000
// > 1000 indicates compaction falling behind
| WHERE attributes.node_id IS NOT NULL AND attributes.store IS NOT NULL
| STATS l0_files = MAX(storage_l0_num_files) BY attributes.node_id, attributes.store, host.name
| SORT l0_files DESC
